target: F64 same-shape tensor add / sub / div

lever.description: Generalize the same-shape DType::F64 elementwise fast path (previously Mul-only, mcqr.2) into eval_same_shape_f64_binop covering Add/Sub/Mul/Div via a monomorphized eval_same_shape_f64_map closure. Iterates paired F64Bits literals with a tight inlined f64 op loop, revalidating output shape through TensorValue::new, instead of the per-element enum/promotion dispatch of binary_literal_op. Non-Add/Sub/Mul/Div primitives and non-F64/malformed literals return None and fall through to the generic path.

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/(add|div)_1k_f64_vec'

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Output elements are produced in the same pairwise input order.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Each output bit is generated by the same scalar f64 +,-,/ expression used by the generic path (lib.rs closures), then stored with to_bits.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: No comparisons or tie-breaking are introduced.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Only same-shape DType::F64 tensors with F64Bits elements take the fast path for Add/Sub/Mul/Div; other primitives, malformed literal kinds, and broadcasted shapes return None and use the generic path.
